LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT CONTINUED FROM MAY-08-2012 TO LAW AND MOTION AT MAY-25-2012, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-16-2013 Continued To Jan-06-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses And Further Responses To Defts' First Supplemental Requests For Production
Off Calendar Aug-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. [No notification re appearances were received by the Court pursuant to LR 8.3(D).] The parties, having already stipulated for the matter to be heard by Pro Tem Judge Riles on June 18, 2013, the Court now rules as follows: Defendants JOHN DEDOMINIC'S, TRADEMARK CAPITAL SERVICES'S, and M HOLDINGS SECURITIES INC.'S Motion to Compel Responses and Further Responses to Defendants' First Supplemental Requests for Production is Granted, in part. Plaintiff did not comply with the Court's order of June 18 by providing courtesy copies of the Opposition papers pursuant to Local Rule 2.6B. Therefore, only Plaintiff's supplemental report was considered and not the opposition. Defendant's motion is partially granted as follows: Category 1 - all documents related to Steven Roth prior to his retention as a litigation consultant (May 2011); Category 2 - all communications with Lucinda Lee related only to the topics in Defendant's supplemental report; Category 3 - all documents from 2004 to the present; Category 4 - all documents from 2004 to the present; and Category 6 - all documents responsive to the request regarding the Son's Trust, should be produced. The Court further orders that documents identified in the privilege log which are Steven Roth communications prior to his retention as litigation consultant are to be produced and all Lucinda Lee communication regarding the three topic indentified in the Defendant's supplemental report are to be produced. The remainder or protected by attorney-client privilege and the attorney work produce doctrine and should not be produced. All documents are to be produced within 15 days of this order. Proposed order submitted by prevailing party. Judge Pro Tem: Elizabeth Riles; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
